summary: Typosquat of @metamask/sdk with postinstall wallet/SSH/env credential stealer
details: Package name `@wagni_bot/metemask-sdk` is a homoglyph/edit-distance typosquat of the legitimate `@metamask/sdk` (transposed letters: `metemask` vs `metamask`), and `index.js` is an empty stub (`module.exports={}`) with no SDK functionality. The sole real behavior is a credential-harvesting `postinstall.js`, auto-executed on every `npm install` via `package.json` `scripts.postinstall: "node postinstall.js"`. The script (a) enumerates the current working directory, home directory, and crypto-wallet config paths for ETH/BTC/SOL private keys (0x[a-fA-F0-9]{64} regex), BIP39 seed-phrase word matches, JSON keystores, `.env` `PRIVATE_KEY` values, and PEM RSA keys; (b) reads every file under `~/.ssh` other than `known_hosts`/`authorized_keys`, capturing `id_rsa`/`id_ed25519` and other private keys; (c) filters `process.env` for keys containing PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/PK and concatenates name=value pairs; and (d) POSTs each batch, together with hostname, username, and cwd, to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint `http://107.161.90.180:7777` over plain HTTP. This is a canonical wallet/credential stealer delivered via a typosquat of a top wallet SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
